title: The Silent Radix: Positional Notation as Ultrametric Tree and the Calculus of Indications as Remedy
description: Positional notation is not merely a convention for writing numbers — it is an ultrametric tree whose place-value columns encode nested cycles of counting. The base, when made explicit, captures a chosen grouping rhythm that structures the numeral as a hierarchy of nested distinctions. The systematic historical errors catalogued here — the C octal bug, the Mars Climate Orbiter, the Likert-scale mean, the Babylonian sexagesimal ambiguity — all arise from a single structural fault: the silent radix. This paper demonstrates that $p$-adic numbers recover the native ultrametric structure, connects the phenomenon to Spencer-Brown's Laws of Form and the calculus of indications, and proposes nine principles for a cyclic-frame quantitative practice that eliminates the silent radix at its root. Formalized in Lean 4 with all theorems proved (0 sorry).
